topic: Module-level test case docs vs minimal seed for AI test-gen cycle in TS monorepo

findings.finding: Modern AI test generation works best with structured but MINIMAL seeds (feature list + acceptance criteria + invariants), not exhaustive scenario specs
findings.detail: Agent elaborates; humans steer. Pattern is 'spec-as-seed' rather than 'spec-as-spec'

findings.finding: Best INPUT formats ranked: (1) bulleted feature list + acceptance criteria, (2) example tables / I-O pairs, (3) Gherkin (only if humans already write it), (4) freeform markdown
AI-generated tests reach equivalent or better branch coverage than manual on standard scenarios, but systematically MISS unusual workflows / business-rule edge cases
findings.detail: Agent tests 'what is', not 'what should be'

findings.finding: Pure 'skip docs' failure mode: tests cluster on happy paths the agent infers from code, leaving cross-module integration and CLI exit-code semantics under-tested
When specs and tests are separate artifacts maintained by different roles, delivery effectiveness is significantly reduced by constant coordination
BDD/Gherkin first approach widely judged to have lost its way — step-definition explosion, no real stakeholder reading them

Real-world pnpm/vitest monorepos use per-package test strategy with unified runner (vitest projects). Different test TYPES per package (CLI = spawn-and-assert, server = handler+contract, shared = unit + property-based, dashboard = component+route) — NOT different methodologies
Property-based testing (fast-check) gives high ROI for pure functions, parsers, serializers, idempotent ops in shared lib

best_practices.practice: Produce a single short test seed (≤1 page per package) with three sections: feature surface (bullet list), invariants (5-10 must-hold statements), known-tricky cases (3-5)
best_practices.rationale: Captures intent without per-scenario duplication. Survives churn because it lives at intent level, not implementation level

best_practices.practice: Use coverage threshold + invariants represented as exit criterion, NOT 'every bullet has a test'
best_practices.rationale: Avoids the trap of test-case enumeration that decays into stale doc

best_practices.practice: Generate CLI feature surface from --help programmatically, snapshot it; CI fails if drifts from seed
best_practices.rationale: Cheap consistency gate; prevents seed becoming stale

best_practices.practice: Add fast-check property-based tests for shared lib invariants
best_practices.rationale: Covers comprehensive correctness goal far more cheaply than enumerated cases

best_practices.practice: Refresh seed only when INTENT changes, not when implementation changes
best_practices.rationale: Decouples seed from churn; keeps maintenance cost low

alternatives.option: Module-by-module test case docs (the user's initial idea)
alternatives.cons: Double-maintenance; goes stale within 2 release cycles; high writing cost
Needs collaborative ritual; ceremony for solo/internal projects
alternatives.verdict: Avoid — fabric has no such stakeholders surfaced

alternatives.option: API contract (OpenAPI / TS types as source of truth)
alternatives.pros: Types already exist — zero authoring cost

alternatives.cons: Misses semantics of what each flag should do
alternatives.verdict: USE for cli — combine with invariants

alternatives.cons: Needs tooling (Cucumber reports etc.); only useful if external readers exist
alternatives.verdict: Skip initially — revisit if external readership appears

alternatives.cons: Doesn't surface intent gaps, only line gaps
alternatives.verdict: USE as the loop's exit criterion

alternatives.pros: Tests invariants not examples; finds edge cases

pitfalls.issue: Docs go stale when no one outside QA reads them
pitfalls.mitigation: Keep seed ≤1 page; co-locate with package; CI gate on --help drift

pitfalls.issue: Trusting integration-test-cycle alone produces tests that pass but don't validate intent
pitfalls.mitigation: Always provide invariants seed; agent will optimize for 'tests pass' otherwise

pitfalls.issue: Treating dashboard and CLI with identical strategies
Letting seed grow into per-test-case enumeration
pitfalls.mitigation: Hard cap ≤1 page; if grows, indicates 'missing invariant' not 'need more bullets'

codebase_gaps.gap: Recent commits (TASK-038 doctor i18n, TASK-039 init_context_missing) suggest evolving CLI/server behaviour — exactly the 'known-tricky cases' that belong in the seed and would be missed by a no-seed cycle
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Capture in known-tricky cases section of seed before running cycle

codebase_gaps.gap: release/v1.8.0-stabilization branch suggests stabilization phase — right time for seed-driven cycle, not docs-first cycle that delays the loop
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Run integration-test-cycle with minimal seed, defer any heavier doc work

sources.title: How to Write a Good Spec for AI Agents — Addy Osmani / O'Reilly
sources.key_takeaway: Minimal seed > detailed spec for AI agents

sources.title: How BDD Lost Its Way — QualityKiosk
sources.key_takeaway: Strongest evidence against full Gherkin specs as input

sources.title: Specification by Example — Wikipedia
sources.key_takeaway: Combined-artifact rationale; separated artifacts hurt delivery

sources.title: Codoid — How Good Are AI Generated Test Cases
sources.key_takeaway: Failure modes of skip-docs cycles: misses business-rule edge cases

sources.title: Vitest Projects guide
sources.key_takeaway: Real monorepos use unified runner with per-package strategy

sources.title: fast-check + vitest
sources.key_takeaway: Property-based testing for shared lib invariants

sources.title: CLI Integration Tests — Florian Rappl
sources.key_takeaway: Spawn-and-assert pattern; --help snapshot for surface drift
